transcript.clean_text: 24 hours before stage six of the jury italia on the mainland the fifth stage have been on sicily mark cavendish keller buen and alma de mar the sprinters dropped on the climb in the middle of the stage demand his team managed to get back on and it would be miles scotson of his train that took the final corner ramon single dam who led him out and the frenchman winning a stage of the gerald italia for the sixth time in his career on stage six he'd be looking to double up the race reaching calabria the mainland and the journey north beginning from palmy an early climb an intermediate sprint and then a very long straight road up the coast would take the riders to scalia for the first time in 22 long years jansforada of czech republic was the winner last time we were here who would succeed him after 192 kilometers a sunny start in the neutral zone a very relaxed looking peloton and that's how it would stay once the flag was waived no attacks in the first 20 k's that until diego rosa was up the road italian rider from the north west of the country racing for eol or cometa went on the attack nobody would follow him and he'd soon have four minutes of a gap the pelican were very relaxed behind not worried about just one man up the front rosa would take three mountains classification points for his troubles looking for intermediate sprint points would be drawn uper androni jokatoli on a rare occasion not wanting to be in the break but philly potalyani would take the points behind arnold demar and binyam gemai fighting it out for the rest of the magiachi klamino points kimai would get the better of the frenchman just about with mikhail marco finishing in third one point back for germaine all three riders from drawn up are back in the peloton so a four-minute gap for diego rosa and a very calm day in prospect active recovery for the riders and a chance to look at the peliton of the jupiter italia for the first time in a while for the citizens of calabria group as well as quickstep and lotto were the teams to work but work in inverted commas really because it wasn't a difficult job to bring back diego rosa at points almost more difficult to leave him hanging out there in front there was a risk of catching him too early but he was allowed to stay up for the second intermediate sprint too the one that brought the bonus seconds three went to him two would go to tagliani who is looking for more intermediate sprint points and a big effort for leonard kemner starting the day in second place at 39 seconds from the mallarosa he'd now be at 38. rosa's adventure would certainly come to an end with 28 kilometers to go a wave to the camera hello to the rest of the bunch 172 riders would be back together a couple of twists and turns with just over 20 kilometers to go kickstarting the moves to the front they become more aggressive inside the final five kilometers everybody jostling for position into a slight headwind finish a couple of roundabouts had stark choices made and inside the final kilometer fresh legs had absolute chaos it was messy left and right there were riders cutting each other up and it'd be a sprint to the line that had hearts in mouths fondly berger was taken out thankfully staying on his bike and the same fate was suffered by fernando gabiria that visible in this shot here on the left-hand side on the other side of the road mikhail merkel launching mark cavendish maybe having to go a little too early because of the fact that van lieber could have been taken out behind ewen came off his wheel and then came demar demar ewan cavendish all together at the line the photo finish was between arnold de mar and caleb ewan and arnold will take the win for a second successive day lots of questions to be asked after the messy sprint dimar finally finding his way out getting the wheel of uh and hitting the front just when it mattered the frenchman winning on the bike throw and on a roll with two from two he'd extend his lead in the maya chiclamino points competition and become the frenchman with the most stage wins at the gyro d'italia in history winning it on the bike throw demar ewan kavadish gyaramai and and turns in the top 10. another day another day on the podium for arno dimar again getting used to this rather like he did at this race two years ago in terms of the general classification no change in positions in the top ten just a second back for leonard kemner who's now 38 seconds from the leader juan pedro lopez perez juan pelopez the man in pink that was stage six stage seven is a different matter one of the hardest stages of the entire italia they call it medium mountain lots of climbs in succession hardly a meter of flat and twisty sinuous roads racing to potenza a lot of climbing only two pure mountain stages have more climbing than this supposedly a medium mountain day it'll be a big day to watch a nervous day for the bunch general classification guys have to be on top join us for the coverage live on gcm plus discovery plus on eurosport
